# Invite Permissions Checklist: Assign Permissions During Invitation

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, consistency, and coverage of requirements for assigning specific permissions to invited users during the invitation process (User Story 4, FR-008, FR-009).
**Created**: 2025-03-08
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are the actors who may assign permissions during invite explicitly defined (e.g. admin, or “authorized to create invites”)? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 4]
- [x] CHK002 - Is the source of the “system’s defined permission set” specified or referenced (e.g. same as role permissions)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK003 - Are requirements for storing permissions on the invite (data model or API payload) documented? [Gap, Spec §Key Entities Invite]
- [x] CHK004 - Is it explicit whether the inviter can assign only a role, only permissions, or both? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 4 Acceptance 1, FR-008]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 - Is the merge rule when both role and explicit permissions are assigned defined unambiguously (union vs override)? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 4 Acceptance 4, FR-009]
- [x] CHK006 - Are “valid, current permissions” defined (e.g. from a fixed enum or API list)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009]
- [x] CHK007 - Is “reject or sanitize” for invalid permission keys specified (reject entire request vs store only valid)? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 4 Acceptance 3]
- [x] CHK008 - Is the behavior when no role and no explicit permissions are assigned specified (e.g. empty permissions, or require at least one)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 - Do User Story 4 acceptance scenarios align with FR-008 and FR-009? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 4, §FR-008, §FR-009]
- [x] CHK010 - Is SC-005 (“effective permissions match what was assigned”) consistent with the defined merge rule? [Consistency, Spec §SC-005]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK011 - Can “user’s effective permissions match what was assigned” (SC-005) be verified from requirements alone? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK012 - Are acceptance criteria for “access only the allowed features” testable (e.g. reference permission list or feature set)? [Measurability, Spec §User Story 4]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 - Are requirements defined for updating an existing invite’s permissions (if supported)? [Coverage, Gap]
- [x] CHK014 - Are requirements specified for listing or displaying assigned permissions on the invite (admin UI)? [Coverage, Gap]
- [x] CHK015 - Is the “permission removed from system after invite created” case fully specified (apply only valid at accept time)? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK016 - Are requirements for invalid or unknown permission keys at invite creation/update explicitly stated? [Edge Case, Spec §User Story 4 Acceptance 3]
- [x] CHK017 - Is the edge case where role is deleted after invite created but before accept addressed? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [x] CHK018 - Are requirements for empty permission list (explicitly assign no permissions) documented? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-008]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK019 - Is the assumption that staff profile supports both role-derived and direct permissions documented? [Assumption, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK020 - Are dependencies on existing roles/permissions APIs (e.g. GET /api/permissions) stated for the invite UI? [Dependency, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant spec sections (e.g. §FR-008) when resolving items
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
